heading: Recordkeeping, investigations, and enforcement

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this chapter, each person who sells, labels, or represents any agricultural product as having been produced or handled using organic methods shall make available to the Secretary or the applicable governing State official, on request by the Secretary or official, all records associated with the agricultural product.

children.label: A
children.text: a detailed history of substances applied to fields or agricultural products;

children.label: B
children.text: the name and address of each person who applied such a substance; and

children.label: C
children.text: the date, rate, and method of application of each such substance.

children.heading: Maintenance of records
children.label: A Maintenance of records
children.text: A certifying agent shall maintain all records concerning the activities of the certifying agent under this chapter for a period of not less than 10 years.

children.heading: Access for Secretary
children.label: B Access for Secretary
children.text: A certifying agent shall provide to the Secretary and the applicable governing State official (or a representative) access to all records concerning the activities of the certifying agent under this chapter.

children.label: i
children.text: transferred to the Secretary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: made available to the applicable governing State official.

children.heading: Unlawful act
children.label: 4 Unlawful act
children.text: It shall be unlawful and a violation of this chapter for any person covered by this chapter to fail or refuse to provide accurate information (including a delay in the timely delivery of such information) required by the Secretary under this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: the identity of all relevant persons (including parties to a contract); and

children.label: B
children.text: proprietary business information.

children.label: A
children.text: to verify the accuracy of any information reported or made available under this chapter; and

children.label: B
children.text: to determine whether a person covered by this chapter has committed a violation of any provision of this chapter, including an order or regulation promulgated by the Secretary pursuant to this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: administer oaths and affirmations;

children.label: B
children.text: subpoena witnesses;

children.label: C
children.text: compel attendance of witnesses;

children.label: D
children.text: take evidence; and

children.label: E
children.text: require the production of any records required to be maintained under this chapter that are relevant to an investigation.

children.heading: Information sharing during active investigation
children.label: 3 Information sharing during active investigation
children.text: In carrying out this chapter, all parties to an active investigation (including certifying agents, State organic certification programs, and the national organic program) shall share confidential business information with Federal Government officers and employees involved in the investigation as necessary to fully investigate and enforce potential violations of this chapter.

children.heading: Misuse of label
children.label: 1 Misuse of label
children.text: Any person who knowingly sells or labels a product as organic, except in accordance with this chapter, shall be subject to a civil penalty of not more than $10,000.

children.heading: False statement
children.label: 2 False statement
children.text: Any person who makes a false statement under this chapter to the Secretary, a governing State official, or a certifying agent shall be punished in accordance with .section 1001 of title 18

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (C), any person that carries out an activity described in subparagraph (B), after notice and an opportunity to be heard, shall not be eligible, for the 5-year period beginning on the date of the occurrence, to receive a certification under this chapter with respect to any farm or handling operation in which the person has an interest.

children.label: i
children.text: making a false statement;

children.label: ii
children.text: attempting to have a label indicating that an agricultural product is organically produced affixed to an agricultural product that a person knows, or should have reason to know, to have been produced or handled in a manner that is not in accordance with this chapter; or

children.label: iii
children.text: otherwise violating the purposes of the applicable organic certification program, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: C Waiver
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), the Secretary may modify or waive a period of ineligibility under this paragraph if the Secretary determines that the modification or waiver is in the best interests of the applicable organic certification program established under this chapter.

children.heading: Reporting of violations
children.label: 4 Reporting of violations
children.text: A certifying agent shall immediately report any violation of this chapter to the Secretary or the applicable governing State official.

children.label: A
children.text: lose accreditation as a certifying agent under this chapter; and

children.label: B
children.text: be ineligible to be accredited as a certifying agent under this chapter for a period of not less than 3 years, beginning on the date of the determination.

children.label: i
children.text: the Federal Meat Inspection Act ( et seq.);21 U.S.C. 601

children.label: ii
children.text: the Poultry Products Inspection Act ( et seq.); or21 U.S.C. 451

children.label: iii
children.text: the Egg Products Inspection Act ( et seq.);21 U.S.C. 1031

children.label: B
children.text: the authority of the Secretary of Health and Human Services under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act ( et seq.); or21 U.S.C. 301

children.label: C
children.text: the authority of the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency under the Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act ( et seq.).7 U.S.C. 136
